title: Change in length
short: How much an object lengthens or shortens when its temperature changes, in metres (m).
full:
The change in length ΔL is the difference between an object's final and initial length caused by thermal expansion or contraction, ΔL = α·L₀·ΔT. It is positive when the object is heated and negative when cooled.

Though usually small, ΔL is why gaps are left in bridges, pipelines and railway tracks — without them the expansion would buckle the structure.

The SI unit is the metre (m).
